The fairing separates and AST SpaceMobile’s BlueBird 7 satellite will deploy to low Earth orbit."},{"url":"https://spaceflightnow.com/2026/04/19/third-flight-of-blue-origins-new-glenn-rocket-to-feature-1st-reuse-of-booster/","title":"Third flight of Blue Origin’s New Glenn rocket to feature 1st reuse of booster – Spaceflight Now","content":"The booster, ‘Never Tell Me the Odds’, previously launched in November 2025 and successfully touched down on the company’s ocean-going landing platform, ‘Jacklyn’.
“We remain on track to achieve our target of deploying 45 to 60 satellites into low Earth orbit by the end of this year,” AST Spacemobile’s Chairman and CEO Abel Avellan said in an earning call in March.
Running more than three months late, a United Launch Alliance Delta 4-Heavy rocket took off Thursday and carried a U.S. government spy satellite into an orbit thousands of miles above Earth in the first mission from the newly-renamed Cape Canaveral Space Force Station."},{"url":"https://spacenews.com/third-new-glenn-launch-suffers-upper-stage-malfunction/","title":"Third New Glenn launch suffers upper stage malfunction - SpaceNews","content":"WASHINGTON — Blue Origin’s New Glenn suffered a malfunction of its second stage on the rocket’s third flight April 19, stranding its payload in an unrecoverable “off-nominal” orbit and dealing the company a setback as it seeks to increase its flight rate.
The rocket’s payload, AST SpaceMobile’s BlueBird 7 satellite, was scheduled to deploy about 75 minutes after liftoff into a 460-kilometer circular orbit at an inclination of 49.4 degrees.
2 more free articles this month."},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_Glenn","title":"New Glenn - Wikipedia","content":"Its first flight, NG-1, took place on January 16, 2025, from Cape Canaveral Launch Complex 36.
By February 2019, several launches for New Glenn had been contracted: five for OneWeb, an unspecified amount of Telesat, one each for Eutelsat, mu Space Corp and SKY Perfect JSAT.
Blue Origin reached orbit on its first attempt, injecting the GS-2 upper stage and the Blue Ring Pathfinder payload into medium earth orbit.
Telemetry showed that the booster was traveling at an approximate speed of Mach 5.5 at an altitude of 84,226 ft (25.7 km) before it was deemed lost.
This made Blue Origin the second company, behind SpaceX and its Falcon rockets, to successfully deploy a spacecraft in orbit while landing the vehicle's booster.
Eutelsat, Thailand startup mu Space Corp and SKY Perfect JSAT have geosynchronous orbit communications satellite launches planned after 2020, while internet satellite constellation fleet operator OneWeb had an agreement by 2018 for five launches.
In November 2024, AST SpaceMobile selected Blue Origin to launch some of its Block 2 satellites.
This launch in April 2026 resulted in a failure of the satellite deployment due to the upper stage malfunction of the New Glenn.
For example, GS1-2‑1 and GS1-2‑2 represent the first and second flights of booster GS1-2, Never Tell Me The Odds."},{"url":"https://satnews.com/2026/02/01/blue-origin-to-validate-first-booster-reuse-on-new-glenn-3-mission-for-ast-spacemobile/","title":"Blue Origin to Validate First Booster Reuse on New Glenn-3 ...","content":"Feb 1, 2026 — On January 22, 2026, Blue Origin announced that its upcoming New Glenn-3 (NG-3) mission will mark a historic double-milestone: the first ...Read more"}]}

New Glenn's third mission lifted off on Sunday, April 19, 2026, from Launch Complex 36 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station. ## NG-3 Flight Profile. New Glenn lifts off from Launch Complex 36. Meanwhile, the two BE-3U engines ignite, propelling New Glenn’s second stage into space. After completion of the mission profile, the second stage will be safed and disposed via controlled ocean reentry, which is compliant with the U.S. Government’s Orbital Debris Mitigation Standard Practices. A diagram of New Glenn 3’s flight profile from liftoff at LC-36 through ascent, stage separation, fairing jettison, and payload separation. ### Blue Origin Images.
